title: Requirements front-matter
description: Front-matter shape for a Requirements artifact. Lives at /specs/{scope}/requirements.md for non-leaf scopes; at /specs/requirements.md for the root scope. Composes the universal envelope and adds the artifact-level identity (scope, parent_scope, derived_from, governing_adrs) that the body's requirement entries inherit. The per-requirement embedded-YAML block shape is defined in $defs/requirement and exported for use by the artifact parser and downstream validators — this schema does not parse or validate body contents itself; it validates only the front-matter YAML. The artifact-level id prefix REQS- is distinct from the per-requirement id prefix REQ- — do not conflate: REQS identifies the document, REQ identifies an individual requirement inside it.

id.description: Artifact identifier. Literal 'REQS' at root scope; 'REQS-{scope-flattened}' otherwise, where scope path segments (normally separated by '/') are joined with hyphens. Example: scope 'session/user-mgmt' → id 'REQS-session-user-mgmt'. This is the artifact-level id; individual requirements inside carry REQ-{…} ids (see $defs/requirement).

artifact_type.description: Artifact-type discriminator, fixed for this schema.

scope.description: Scope path this Requirements artifact covers. Empty string denotes the root scope. Kebab-case segments separated by '/'.

parent_scope.description: Parent scope path. Null at root (no parent). At branches: the containing scope's path.

derived_from.description: Upstream artifact ids this Requirements document is refined from. At root: [PB]. At non-root branches: typically [REQS-<parent>] plus any inherited constraints. Non-empty list; empty would mean an orphan artifact.

governing_adrs.description: Cross-cutting ADRs that constrain this scope. Optional; empty or absent when the scope inherits no ADR-level constraints.

requirement.description: Per-requirement block shape — the YAML an embedded requirement entry in the markdown body renders to. Exported for use by parsers and validators extracting individual requirement blocks from a Requirements document. Not enforced by the top-level schema on the front-matter object itself. Rationale is required and non-empty; the retrofit discipline narrows rationale_recovery_status to 'verified' or 'unknown' only — the 'reconstructed' value is nonsensical for a human-only field (no-fabrication rule).

id.description: Requirement identifier. Pattern REQ-{scope-or-seq}. Examples: 'REQ-001' at root; 'REQ-session-042'; 'REQ-app-checkout-017'. Lowercase kebab-case throughout.

statement.description: The requirement itself. Atomic (one 'shall'), testable, solution-free. Non-empty string.

rationale.description: Why this requirement exists — the defensible 'why' that survives pressure to drop or rework it. Human-only field: must be authored by a human or explicitly marked unknown in retrofit (see rationale_recovery_status). Non-empty string.

acceptance.description: Acceptance criteria clarifying test conditions when the statement alone is ambiguous. Prose (commonly Given/When/Then). Optional.

derived_from.description: Per-requirement lineage: upstream user story, stakeholder need, parent requirement, or inherited constraint this requirement was refined from. Optional — when absent, the artifact-level derived_from provides lineage.

derivation.description: Optional marker explaining how this requirement came to be (for example 'derived' when it was derived from an inherited constraint rather than directly authored). Free-text; no closed enum — tighten only when a stable vocabulary emerges.

statement_recovery_status.description: Retrofit provenance of the statement field specifically. Behaviour-side field: observable from code and tests. Allowed values: 'verified' (human confirmed), 'reconstructed' (inferred from code/test evidence), 'unknown'.

rationale_recovery_status.description: Retrofit provenance of the rationale field specifically. Human-only: the 'reconstructed' value is banned by the no-fabrication rule. Allowed values: 'verified' or 'unknown' only.

follow_up.description: Open actions to close gaps surfaced by retrofit or authoring (missing rationale, pending stakeholder confirmation, unresolved constraint). Optional; empty or absent when no follow-ups are tracked.

owner.description: Person, team, or role responsible for closing this follow-up.
